meta.title: Rescue of Sendai virus cDNA templates with cDNA clones expressing parainfluenza virus type 3 N, P and L proteins

abstractHtml: Several years ago, we reported that a Sendai virus (SeV) defective genome (DIH4UV) could be rescued in vivo with human parainfluenza virus type 1 (hPIV1) and bovine PIV3 but not by measles virus or vesicular stomatitis virus. It was concluded that the cis-acting RNA sequences were conserved within the SeV/PIV1/PIV3 group but that interactions between the polymerase complex (P-L) and the template protein N were unique for each virus. We have re-examined these conclusions using proteins expressed from cloned N, P and L genes for SeV and PIV3. The results demonstrate the specificity of the protein-protein interactions between polymerase and template, and confirm the prediction of the earlier work that PIV3 N, P and L proteins are capable of assembling and replicating SeV minigenomes also expressed from a cDNA clone.
